finding_results.notes: Replaced 'Dan felt something open in his chest that was specific to this room' with Dan's established perceptual mode: the bourbon glass gaining weight in his hand, the algorithmic jazz surfacing through his attention, and specific visual details of Mira (tortoiseshell clip, collarbones, the gap in her back teeth) filling his entire field of attention. The emotional payload ('the precise, localized hurt of being reminded...') now arrives through the accumulation of concrete sensory detail rather than an interior chest-opening. Also addressed a secondary sternum reference at line 249 ('Dan felt them in his sternum') by rerouting through concrete touch — the leather seat grain under his palm — consistent with the same perceptual pattern. No chest, sternum, rib, or interior-cavity reference remains in either passage. Both replacements are consistent with Dan's stress-tell pattern (grabbing onto concrete physical detail when the abstract becomes unbearable) and maintain voice distinction from Mira's somatic register.

summary: Single finding (F04, MEDIUM) fully addressed. Removed the chest-as-emotion-container construction at line 181 and a secondary sternum reference at line 249, rerouting Dan's emotional recognition through his established perceptual mode — concrete physical objects (bourbon glass weight, condensation, seat leather grain) and external sensory attention (algorithmic jazz, visual details of Mira). Voice distinction between Dan and Mira chapters is strengthened: Dan now processes peak emotion through external surfaces and sensory inventory, while Mira's somatic register remains her own. No structural changes; all surrounding text preserved.
